13	// resolveTsnetAuthKey applies auth key precedence:
14	// TS_AUTHKEY env var > --tsnet-auth-key CLI flag > config file auth_key.
15	// The env var is preferred because CLI flags are visible in ps/history.
16	func resolveTsnetAuthKey(envKey, flagKey, configKey string) string {
17		if envKey != "" {
18			return envKey
19		}
20		if flagKey != "" {
21			return flagKey
22		}
23		return configKey
24	}
25	
26	// defaultTsnetHostname returns "ddx-<short-hostname>" for the current host,
27	// falling back to "ddx" when the hostname is unavailable or empty.
28	func defaultTsnetHostname() string {
29		h, err := os.Hostname()
30		if err != nil || h == "" {
31			return "ddx"
32		}
33		// Trim any trailing domain so the ts-net name stays short and stable.
34		if i := strings.Index(h, "."); i >= 0 {
35			h = h[:i]
36		}
37		return "ddx-" + h
38	}

40	func (f *CommandFactory) newServerCommand() *cobra.Command {
41		var port int
42		var addr string
43		var tlsCert string
44		var tlsKey string
45		var tsnetEnabled bool
46		var tsnetHostname string
47		var tsnetAuthKey string
48		var hubMode bool
49		var federationAllowPlainHTTP bool
50		var hubURL string
51		var federationSelfURL string
52	
53		cmd := &cobra.Command{
54			Use:   "server",
55			Short: "Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server",
56			Long: `Start the DDx server exposing documents, beads, document graph,
57	and agent session logs over HTTP REST and MCP endpoints.
58	
59	HTTP API:
60	  GET /api/health              Liveness check
61	  GET /api/ready               Readiness check with dependency status
62	  GET /api/documents           List library documents (?type=)
63	  GET /api/documents/:path     Read a document
64	  GET /api/search?q=           Full-text search across documents
65	  GET /api/personas/:role      Resolve persona for a role
66	  GET /api/beads               List beads (?status=&label=)
67	  GET /api/beads/:id           Show a specific bead
68	  GET /api/beads/ready         List ready beads
69	  GET /api/beads/blocked       List blocked beads
70	  GET /api/beads/status        Bead summary counts
71	  GET /api/beads/dep/tree/:id  Dependency tree for a bead
72	  GET /api/docs/graph          Document dependency graph
73	  GET /api/docs/stale          Stale documents
74	  GET /api/docs/:id            Document metadata and staleness
75	  GET /api/docs/:id/deps       Upstream dependencies
76	  GET /api/docs/:id/dependents Downstream dependents
77	  GET /api/agent/sessions      List agent sessions (?harness=&since=)
78	  GET /api/agent/sessions/:id  Session detail
79	  GET /api/metrics/summary     Process metrics summary (?since=)
80	  GET /api/metrics/cost        Process metrics cost (?bead=&feature=&since=)
81	  GET /api/metrics/cycle-time  Process metrics cycle time (?since=)
82	  GET /api/metrics/rework      Process metrics rework (?since=)
83	
84	MCP (POST /mcp):
85	  ddx_list_documents           List library documents
86	  ddx_read_document            Read a document
87	  ddx_search                   Full-text search
88	  ddx_resolve_persona          Resolve persona for a role
89	  ddx_list_beads               List beads
90	  ddx_show_bead                Show a specific bead
91	  ddx_bead_ready               List ready beads
92	  ddx_bead_status              Bead summary counts
93	  ddx_doc_graph                Document dependency graph
94	  ddx_doc_stale                Stale documents
95	  ddx_doc_show                 Document metadata
96	  ddx_doc_deps                 Upstream dependencies
97	  ddx_agent_sessions           List agent sessions`,
98			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
99				if federationAllowPlainHTTP && !hubMode {
100					return fmt.Errorf("--federation-allow-plain-http requires --hub-mode")
101				}
102				listenAddr := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", addr, port)
103				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx server listening on https://%s\n", listenAddr)
104				srv := server.New(listenAddr, f.WorkingDir)
105				if hubMode {
106					srv.EnableHubMode(federationAllowPlainHTTP)
107					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx hub-mode enabled (federation routes mounted)\n")
108					if federationAllowPlainHTTP {
109						fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "WARNING: --federation-allow-plain-http is ON; non-ts-net peers may register\n")
110					}
111				}
112	
113				if strings.TrimSpace(hubURL) != "" {
114					selfURL := federationSelfURL
115					if selfURL == "" {
116						selfURL = "https://" + listenAddr
117					}
118					if err := srv.EnableSpokeMode(cmd.Context(), hubURL, selfURL); err != nil {
119						return fmt.Errorf("federation spoke registration failed: %w", err)
120					}
121					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx spoke-mode enabled (registered with hub %s as %s)\n", hubURL, selfURL)
122				}
123	
124				// Build tsnet config. tsnet is on by default — flags override config,
125				// and --tsnet=false disables it. Hostname defaults to ddx-<hostname>.
126				tc := &config.TsnetConfig{Enabled: true}
127				if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir); err == nil && cfg.Server != nil && cfg.Server.Tsnet != nil {
128					*tc = *cfg.Server.Tsnet
129				}
130				if cmd.Flags().Changed("tsnet") {
131					tc.Enabled = tsnetEnabled
132				}
133				if tsnetHostname != "" {
134					tc.Hostname = tsnetHostname
135				}
136				if tc.Hostname == "" {
137					tc.Hostname = defaultTsnetHostname()
138				}
139				// Prefer TS_AUTHKEY env var; CLI flag is a fallback (secrets on CLI are visible in ps/history)
140				tc.AuthKey = resolveTsnetAuthKey(os.Getenv("TS_AUTHKEY"), tsnetAuthKey, tc.AuthKey)
141				if tc.Enabled {
142					srv.TsnetConfig = tc
143					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "DDx ts-net enabled (hostname: %s)\n", tc.Hostname)
144				}
145	
146				return srv.ListenAndServeTLS(tlsCert, tlsKey)
147			},
148		}
149	
150		cmd.Flags().IntVarP(&port, "port", "p", 7743, "Port to listen on")
151		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&addr, "addr", "127.0.0.1", "Address to bind to")
152		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&tlsCert, "tls-cert", "", "TLS certificate file (PEM); auto-generates self-signed cert if omitted")

158		cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&federationAllowPlainHTTP, "federation-allow-plain-http", false, "Accept federation registrations from non-loopback non-ts-net peers (hub-mode only; logs WARN on each accepted plain-HTTP registration)")
159		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&hubURL, "hub", "", "Federation hub base URL to register with on startup (e.g. https://hub:7743). Enables spoke-mode (FEAT-026). Combine with --hub-mode for hub_spoke role.")
160		cmd.Flags().StringVar(&federationSelfURL, "federation-self-url", "", "Self-URL advertised to the hub when --hub is set; defaults to https://<listen-addr>")
161	
162		// Worker management
163		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerWorkersCommand())
164	
165		// State file utilities (prune, …)
166		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStateCommand())
167	
168		// Service management (systemd on Linux, launchd on macOS)
169		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerInstallCommand())
170		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerUninstallCommand())
171		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStartCommand())
172		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStopCommand())
173		cmd.AddCommand(f.newServerStatusCommand())
174	
175		return cmd
176	}

1	import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
2	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
3	import { gql } from 'graphql-request'
4	
5	export const FEDERATION_NODES_QUERY = gql`
6		query FederationNodes {
7			federationNodes {
8				id
9				nodeId
10				name
11				url
12				status
13				ddxVersion
14				schemaVersion
15				capabilities
16				registeredAt
17				lastHeartbeat
18				writeCapability
19				lastError
20			}
21		}
22	`
23	
24	export interface FederationNode {
25		id: string
26		nodeId: string
27		name: string
28		url: string
29		status: string
30		ddxVersion: string
31		schemaVersion: string
32		capabilities: string[]
33		registeredAt: string
34		lastHeartbeat: string | null
35		writeCapability: boolean
36		lastError: string | null
37	}
38	
39	interface QueryResult {
40		federationNodes: FederationNode[]
41	}
42	
43	export const load: PageLoad = async ({ fetch }) => {
44		const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
45		try {
46			const data = await client.request<QueryResult>(FEDERATION_NODES_QUERY)
47			return { nodes: data.federationNodes, error: null as string | null }
48		} catch (e) {
49			return { nodes: [] as FederationNode[], error: (e as Error).message }
50		}
51	}

1	package server
2	
3	// B14.8a: Backend chaos / integration suite for federation. Each subtest
4	// exercises one failure scenario end-to-end against the real hub HTTP
5	// routes (via Server.Handler) and real spoke servers (httptest.Server),
6	// asserting both behavior (caller sees partial result, no panic, others
7	// keep working) AND the resulting per-spoke status transitions in the
8	// hub registry.
9	//
10	// Scenarios (per bead AC):
11	//   1. hub crash mid-fan-out      → caller sees partial result, no panic
12	//   2. stale spoke during query   → marked stale, not blocking
13	//   3. duplicate registration     → idempotent (same identity) + 409 (conflicting identity)
14	//   4. newer-version registration → degraded status recorded
15	//   5. slow-spoke timeout         → per-node timeout enforced, others succeed
16	//   6. hub restart + immediate    → registry rebuilt from disk + repopulated by next heartbeat
17	//
18	// All scenarios are deterministic: no time.Sleep gates correctness; only
19	// per-node deadlines (which fire in milliseconds) and explicit status
20	// reconcile calls drive transitions.
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"net/http"
26		"net/http/httptest"
27		"os"
28		"path/filepath"
29		"testing"
30		"time"
31	
32		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"
33	)
34	
35	// chaosSpoke pairs an httptest.Server with the SpokeRecord that the hub
36	// would have registered for it, so each scenario can either drive
37	// register-via-HTTP or seed the registry directly.
38	type chaosSpoke struct {
39		srv *httptest.Server
40		rec federation.SpokeRecord
41	}
42	
43	func newChaosSpoke(t *testing.T, nodeID string, handler http.HandlerFunc) *chaosSpoke {
44		t.Helper()
45		mux := http.NewServeMux()
46		mux.HandleFunc("/graphql", handler)
47		srv := httptest.NewServer(mux)
48		t.Cleanup(srv.Close)
49		return &chaosSpoke{
50			srv: srv,
51			rec: federation.SpokeRecord{
52				NodeID:        nodeID,
53				Name:          nodeID,
54				URL:           srv.URL,
55				DDxVersion:    "0.10.5",
56				SchemaVersion: federation.CurrentSchemaVersion,
57				Capabilities:  []string{"beads", "runs"},
58				Status:        federation.StatusActive,
59			},
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// registerVia drives a real /api/federation/register call so we exercise
64	// the full HTTP path (handshake, persistence, status assignment).
65	func registerVia(t *testing.T, s *Server, sp *chaosSpoke, identityFP string) *http.Response {
66		t.Helper()
67		body := map[string]any{
68			"node_id":              sp.rec.NodeID,
69			"identity_fingerprint": identityFP,
70			"name":                 sp.rec.Name,
71			"url":                  sp.rec.URL,
72			"ddx_version":          sp.rec.DDxVersion,
73			"schema_version":       sp.rec.SchemaVersion,
74			"capabilities":         sp.rec.Capabilities,
75		}
76		return federationDoRequest(t, s, "POST", "/api/federation/register", body, "loopback")
77	}
78	
79	// statusOf reads the current hub-side registry status for one spoke.
80	func statusOf(t *testing.T, s *Server, nodeID string) federation.SpokeStatus {
81		t.Helper()
82		snap := s.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot()
83		if snap == nil {
84			t.Fatalf("registry snapshot nil (hub mode not enabled?)")
85		}
86		if rec := snap.FindSpoke(nodeID); rec != nil {
87			return rec.Status
88		}
89		t.Fatalf("spoke %q absent from registry", nodeID)
90		return ""
91	}
92	
93	// applyStatusUpdates writes fan-out side-effects back into the hub
94	// registry the way a production caller would.
95	func applyStatusUpdates(t *testing.T, s *Server, res *federation.FanOutResult) {
96		t.Helper()
97		s.hub.mu.Lock()
98		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
99		for nodeID, st := range res.StatusUpdates {
100			if err := s.hub.registry.SetStatus(nodeID, st); err != nil {

sample.md
# Playwright e2e fixture workspace

This directory provides a self-contained DDx workspace that the default
Playwright harness copies into a temp directory before booting the Go DDx
server. The fixture isolates e2e runs from the developer's home directory and
from the repository's live `.ddx/` state.

Contents:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so the bead API endpoints
  return non-empty data.
- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` — minimal personas, prompts, and templates so the document
  and persona endpoints have something to list.
- `docs/` — small docs library for the document graph endpoint.

The fixture is read-only; the harness copies it to a `mktemp -d` workspace at
boot so test runs do not mutate these files. Do not reference user-home paths
from anything in this directory.

1	// Quorum review (multi-model review) end-to-end UI flow.
2	//
3	// Driving spec for the quorum-review UI surfaced from
4	// `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b,c`. The flow under test:
5	//
6	//   1. Operator opens a bead (or artifact) and dispatches a quorum review,
7	//      picking ≥2 harnesses and a verdict policy (majority / unanimous).
8	//   2. Server fans out to each harness and streams per-model findings.
9	//   3. UI shows a per-arm column with verdict + structured findings, plus a
10	//      rollup verdict computed from the policy.
11	//   4. Operator can drill into a single arm's raw output and link back to the
12	//      originating bead / artifact.
13	//
14	// The quorum-review view is not yet implemented (PRD §437 "Multi-agent review
15	// workflow produces structured findings from quorum"). These tests are checked
16	// in as a TDD seed: every case is marked `test.fixme` so CI stays green; once
17	// the view ships, drop the fixme markers one case at a time as features land.
18	// Mocks below describe the GraphQL contract the view is expected to consume.
19

{
	"name": "frontend",
	"private": true,
	"version": "0.0.1",
	"type": "module",
	"scripts": {
		"dev": "vite dev",
		"build": "vite build",
		"preview": "vite preview",
		"prepare": "playwright install && svelte-kit sync || echo ''",
		"check": "svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json",
		"check:watch": "svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json --watch",
		"lint": "prettier --check . && eslint .",
		"format": "prettier --write .",
		"test:unit": "vitest",

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"

12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"
13	)
14	
15	// HubDefaultDDxVersion is the ddx_version the hub advertises when validating
16	// spoke handshakes. It is exposed as a field so tests and operators can pin
17	// the hub's view of "current ddx version" without rebuilding the binary.
18	const HubDefaultDDxVersion = "0.1.0"
19	
20	// federationHub holds the per-server state for hub-mode federation: the
21	// in-memory registry, the disk path used to persist it, the warning logger
22	// (for plain-HTTP accepted registrations), and configuration knobs.
23	type federationHub struct {
24		mu             sync.Mutex
25		registry       *federation.FederationRegistry
26		statePath      string // empty disables disk persistence
27		allowPlainHTTP bool
28		hubDDxVersion  string
29		hubSchemaVer   string
30		warnLog        func(format string, args ...any)
31		warnings       []string // captured plain-HTTP warnings (test introspection)
32		conflictsLog   []string // captured 409 conflict log lines (test introspection)
33		now            func() time.Time
34	}
35	
36	// EnableHubMode mounts the /api/federation/* routes on this server, switches
37	// the hub-mode flag on, and (best effort) loads the registry from disk so a
38	// hub restart can rebuild state from a prior session.
39	//
40	// Must be called before ListenAndServe / ListenAndServeTLS. Calling it more
41	// than once is a no-op after the first call.
42	//
43	// allowPlainHTTP relaxes the default ts-net/loopback peer policy on the
44	// federation routes — non-trusted peers are accepted, but each accepted
45	// registration emits a WARN log entry. This is the operator opt-out for
46	// federations spanning networks where ts-net is not viable.
47	func (s *Server) EnableHubMode(allowPlainHTTP bool) {
48		if s.hub != nil {
49			return
50		}
51		statePath, err := federation.DefaultStatePath()
52		if err != nil {
53			statePath = ""
54		}
55		var reg *federation.FederationRegistry
56		if statePath != "" {
57			if loaded, lerr := federation.LoadStateFrom(statePath); lerr == nil {
58				reg = loaded
59			}
60		}
61		if reg == nil {
62			reg = federation.NewRegistry()
63		}
64		s.hub = &federationHub{
65			registry:       reg,
66			statePath:      statePath,
67			allowPlainHTTP: allowPlainHTTP,
68			hubDDxVersion:  HubDefaultDDxVersion,
69			hubSchemaVer:   federation.CurrentSchemaVersion,
70			warnLog:        func(format string, args ...any) { log.Printf("WARN: "+format, args...) },
71			now:            time.Now,
72		}
73		s.HubMode = true
74	
75		gate := func(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return s.requireFederationTrusted(h) }
76		s.route("POST /api/federation/register", gate(s.handleFederationRegister))
77		s.route("POST /api/federation/heartbeat", gate(s.handleFederationHeartbeat))
78		s.route("GET /api/federation/spokes", gate(s.handleFederationListSpokes))
79		s.route("DELETE /api/federation/spokes/{id}", gate(s.handleFederationDeregister))
80	}
81	
82	// HubFederationRegistrySnapshot returns a copy of the current registry, for
83	// tests and operator introspection.
84	func (s *Server) HubFederationRegistrySnapshot() *federation.FederationRegistry {
85		if s.hub == nil {
86			return nil
87		}
88		s.hub.mu.Lock()
89		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
90		out := federation.NewRegistry()
91		out.SchemaVersion = s.hub.registry.SchemaVersion
92		out.Spokes = append(out.Spokes, s.hub.registry.Spokes...)
93		return out
94	}
95

97	// captured since the server started. Test-only helper.

100			return nil

data.text: The bead requires a Playwright e2e suite that spawns 2 federation nodes and asserts UI behavior. Investigating the prerequisites revealed a missing precondition in production code: the GraphQL `FederationProvider` is never wired into the resolver outside tests, so the `/federation` page returns no nodes regardless of registry state. Given that, plus the multi-subsystem AC mix (UI tests + ts-net guard + browser orchestration of two server processes), I'm decomposing rather than attempting partial work.

DDx v0.6.2-alpha6-dirty
Commit: edbe72e9

ID:       ddx-e59bff83
Title:    B14.8b: Playwright e2e - 2-node federation flows + ts-net guard AC
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, story:14
Owner:    ddx
Parent:   ddx-a038a090
Deps:     ddx-e869a89c, ddx-13e19750
Desc:     Playwright e2e suite spinning up 2 ddx-server processes (1 hub + 1 spoke) on loopback. Cover: /federation page renders both nodes; fan-out combined views with ?scope=federation show data from both; scope toggle switches LOCAL vs FEDERATION; node badges reflect status; stop spoke and assert offline badge appears within fan-out timeout window; restart spoke and assert returns to active. Plus ts-net guard AC: starting hub WITHOUT --federation-allow-plain-http and a spoke registering over plain HTTP from a non-loopback simulated peer is rejected.
Accept:   e2e spec spawns 2-node federation. /federation page passes assertion. scope=federation toggle exercised. Stop-spoke produces offline badge. Restart-spoke returns active. ts-net guard test: plain-HTTP non-loopback registration rejected without opt-out flag; accepted with --federation-allow-plain-http (and WARN log captured). Tests run under cli/internal/server/frontend/ Playwright config.

input.command:
ddx bead create --title "B14.8b-pre: Wire FederationProvider into hub-mode GraphQL resolver" --type task --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,story:14" --description "Production-code wiring missing as of B14.8b: the GraphQL Resolver's Federation field is never assigned in cli/internal/server/server.go (the only assignment lives in resolver_federation_test.go). As a result, federationNodes/federatedBeads/federatedRuns/federatedProjects always return [] when the server is run with --hub-mode, and the /federation page renders no rows even when spokes are registered.

Add an adapter on *Server that satisfies graphql.FederationProvider (Spokes() and FanOut(ctx, req)), and wire it into the Resolver only when s.hub != nil. The adapter should:
- Return a defensive copy of registry spokes from s.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot().
- Use a single federation.NewFanOutClient() (with hubDDxVersion/hubSchemaVer pinned) and apply StatusUpdates back to the registry under s.hub.mu so subsequent /federation reads observe offline transitions.

Add a Go-level integration test that boots Server.New + EnableHubMode, registers a spoke via /api/federation/register, and asserts that an HTTP POST to /graphql with { federationNodes { nodeId status } } returns the spoke (i.e. the production wiring path actually flows through to the resolver)." --acceptance "1. internal/server/server.go assigns Federation: <adapter> to ddxgraphql.Resolver only when s.hub != nil (no panic / nil-deref when hub mode is off).
2. New adapter type in internal/server (or internal/server/federation_provider.go) implements graphql.FederationProvider; Spokes() returns a defensive copy; FanOut applies StatusUpdates to s.hub.registry under s.hub.mu.
3. New integration test (internal/server/federation_resolver_test.go or similar) boots a hub Server, registers a spoke, posts the federationNodes GraphQL query, and asserts the spoke node_id appears.
4. go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... passes." 2>&1 | tail -10

input.command:
ddx bead create "B14.8b-pre: Wire FederationProvider into hub-mode GraphQL resolver" --type task --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,story:14" --parent ddx-a038a090 --description "Production wiring gap discovered while attempting B14.8b (Playwright e2e). The graphql.Resolver.Federation field is never assigned in internal/server/server.go (only in resolver_federation_test.go). Consequence: federationNodes/federatedBeads/federatedRuns/federatedProjects always return [] when running with --hub-mode. /federation page shows no rows even when spokes are registered, blocking any e2e test that asserts UI federation state.

Add an adapter on *Server satisfying graphql.FederationProvider (Spokes(), FanOut(ctx, req)) and wire it into the Resolver only when s.hub != nil. Adapter contract:
- Spokes() returns a defensive copy of registry spokes (via locked read of s.hub.registry).
- FanOut() runs federation.NewFanOutClient() with hubDDxVersion/hubSchemaVer pinned from s.hub, applies StatusUpdates back to s.hub.registry under s.hub.mu so subsequent reads observe offline transitions, and persists registry changes via persistFederationLocked.

Add a Go integration test that boots Server.New + EnableHubMode, registers a spoke via /api/federation/register, posts { federationNodes { nodeId status } } to /graphql, and asserts the spoke is returned (proving the production wiring path)." --acceptance "1. internal/server/server.go injects Federation: <adapter> into ddxgraphql.Resolver only when s.hub != nil (nil-safe when hub mode is off).
2. Adapter type implements graphql.FederationProvider; Spokes returns defensive copy; FanOut applies StatusUpdates under s.hub.mu and persists registry.
3. Integration test posts federationNodes query and asserts registered spoke surfaces.
4. go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... is green." 2>&1 | tail -5
